commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3c785d08b3653d08e9c0ac591b5625d0e3601beb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Memory Core doctor migration and host SDK importer to move legacy memory sidecar indexes, including vector rows, into per-agent SQLite, with regression tests and SDK surface budget updates.

PR surface: Source +443, Tests +292, Other 0. Total +735 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection plus the linked field report show v2026.6.9 and current main using the per-agent SQLite DB while legacy sidecars can remain populated and unimported.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review remains needed because the PR changes shipped persistent state and public SDK surface, has a blocking archive-on-skip defect, and still needs contributor real setup proof.

target.title: fix #95495: [Bug]: 2026.6.9 silently relocates memory store with no migration, forcing a full re-embed (1499 files) with zero upgrade-time warning
target.body:
## Summary

- Migrates legacy Memory Core sidecar indexes from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` into the per-agent SQLite database through a doctor state migration.
- Keeps normal memory runtime startup scoped to the canonical per-agent database instead of importing old sidecar rows during schema startup.
- Restores legacy `chunks_vec` semantic vector rows into `memory_index_chunks_vec` before handled sidecars are archived.
- Revalidated the branch after syncing to `origin/main` `e66aa357f8`; the remaining diff is limited to 7 files in Memory Core, memory-host SDK exports/schema/tests, and the SDK surface budget script.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this updates the existing issue fix PR, not a sibling follow-up to a merged change.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** OpenClaw 2026.6.9 moved the memory index store from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` to `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agent>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`, but the upgrade path initialized the new per-agent schema without importing the old sidecar rows.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch gives legacy sidecar import one explicit persisted-state owner: the Memory Core doctor state migration. `ensureMemoryIndexSchema()` stays responsible for canonical schema creation for the database it is given; doctor migration handles the one-time sidecar-to-agent import and archive.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users upgrading with existing embedded memory indexes can keep indexed memory state instead of paying for a full re-embed, while users who intentionally clear/rebuild the canonical per-agent index do not have stale sidecar rows resurrected by ordinary runtime startup.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Memory Core doctor state migrations own legacy persisted-state movement. The memory-host schema helper owns row-level import mechanics after the migration has selected a specific legacy sidecar and canonical agent database.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR does not change provider routing, embedding generation, memory search ranking, session transcript storage, channel delivery, or the normal runtime database selection contract.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause persisted-state migration ownership fix.
- **Related PR scan:** `check-upstream-fixed` was rerun after the main sync at head `c2434660695d68b59f2291ebd45603204055a142`; `origin/main` `e66aa357f85b46b5d370efe181cd005a75fbab22` does not contain an equivalent fix (`upstream_fixed: false`, `tree_equivalent_to_main: false`).

## What Changed

- Added `importLegacyMemorySidecarIndex()` in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts:428` and re-exported it through `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts` and `src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts` for the doctor migration path.
- Added doctor state migration `memory-core-legacy-sidecar-index-to-agent-sqlite` in `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts:437`.
- Loads sqlite-vec during the doctor migration, then creates the canonical vector table from legacy vector dimensions when legacy `chunks_vec` rows exist.
- Copies matching legacy `chunks_vec` rows into `memory_index_chunks_vec` through `copyLegacyMemoryVectorRows()` in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts:230` before archive.
- Adds regression coverage for schema startup not importing sidecars at `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts:73`.
- Adds doctor migration coverage for explicit import, already-populated canonical preservation, and vector-backed search at `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts:433` and `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts:452`.
- Updates `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` so the SDK surface budget matches current `origin/main` plus this PR's one public/callable sidecar import export.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Focused regression coverage exists with sqlite-vec enabled and non-empty vector-backed search after doctor migration. The test imports legacy `chunks_vec`, restores `memory_index_chunks_vec`, and verifies vector search returns `chunk-1`.
- **RF-002:** A redacted real `openclaw update` or user-visible doctor plus memory status/search proof was not run in this environment. The proof boundary below explicitly states the unavailable environment and completed local verification.
- **RF-003:** Same as RF-002: the requested real upgrade/doctor/status/search proof is still unavailable here, and the completed proof is local source-level upgrade-state proof with real SQLite files and sqlite-vec search.
- **RF-004:** The archive risk is addressed by restoring matching legacy `chunks_vec` rows into canonical `memory_index_chunks_vec` before the sidecar archive path.
- **RF-005:** The new public plugin SDK callable remains intentionally limited to the explicit Memory Core doctor migration path; maintainer acceptance of that SDK boundary is still a review decision.
- **RF-006:** Contributor proof remains local temporary-state proof rather than a packaged beta upgrade or reporter-state proof; this limitation is disclosed under `What was not tested`.
- **RF-007:** Manual review remains needed for the external proof boundary and SDK boundary decision. The previously reported vector-row migration defect is addressed in this update.
- **RF-008:** Vector rows are restored before archiving sidecars by loading sqlite-vec in doctor migration, creating the canonical vec table from legacy dimensions, and copying matching legacy `chunks_vec` rows during explicit sidecar import.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts`, and `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A legacy sidecar beside an empty canonical per-agent database must stay untouched during schema startup; explicit doctor migration must import sidecar rows into the per-agent database and archive the sidecar; a sidecar with legacy vector rows must restore canonical sqlite-vec rows and support vector-backed search; an already-populated canonical database must remain authoritative.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests use real `node:sqlite` databases, sqlite-vec loading, and the actual schema/migration/search code paths, so they cover the missing-import upgrade path, stale-sidecar startup boundary, and vector-row archive risk without mocking the storage layer.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:test
pnpm tsgo:test:packages
git diff --check && git status --short && git ls-files -u
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Upgrading from the legacy memory sidecar store can preserve existing memory index rows through an explicit doctor state migration, including legacy vector rows, while normal schema startup does not import stale sidecar state into an empty canonical per-agent database.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at current head `c2434660695d68b59f2291ebd45603204055a142` after syncing to `origin/main` `e66aa357f85b46b5d370efe181cd005a75fbab22`, using real `node:sqlite` databases in temporary state directories, sqlite-vec loading, and the Memory Core schema/doctor migration paths.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95631
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:test
pnpm tsgo:test:packages
git diff --check && git status --short && git ls-files -u
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 209.98s
Unit shard: 1 test file passed, 7 tests passed
Tooling shard: 1 test file passed, 5 tests passed
Extension-memory shard: 1 test file passed, 6 tests passed
```

```text
pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check
public package SDK entrypoints:
  entrypoints: 321
  exports: 10338
  callable exports: 5191
  deprecated exports: 3247
  deprecated callable exports: 1601
  unique entrypoint-qualified exports: 10338
package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0
```

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check ... && git diff --check
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check && git status --short && git ls-files -u
(no output)

pnpm tsgo:test
exit 0

pnpm tsgo:test:packages
exit 0
```

```text
check-upstream-fixed after sync
head: c2434660695d68b59f2291ebd45603204055a142
origin_main: e66aa357f85b46b5d370efe181cd005a75fbab22
merge_base: e66aa357f85b46b5d370efe181cd005a75fbab22
upstream_fixed: false
tree_equivalent_to_main: false
result: CONTINUE_ALLOWED
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The doctor migration imports legacy sidecar rows into the per-agent SQLite database, restores migrated `chunks_vec` rows into `memory_index_chunks_vec`, returns the migrated chunk through vector-backed search, and archives the sidecar only after successful handling. If the per-agent index already has rows, doctor migration preserves canonical rows and archives the stale sidecar. Normal schema startup leaves an empty canonical database empty even when an old sidecar exists. The branch now rebases cleanly on `origin/main` `e66aa357f8` with no unmerged index entries.

- **What was not tested:** Not run: packaged release-beta upgrade install, redacted real `openclaw update`, user-visible doctor plus memory status/search terminal proof, external embedding provider, or the reporter's original 1499-file state directory. The proof here is local source-level upgrade-state proof with real SQLite files and sqlite-vec search, not third-party live proof.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, session-state, automation, public SDK surface, and provider-data columns.
- **Risk explanation:** Compatibility and session-state are the intended migration surface: legacy memory index state moves into the canonical per-agent SQLite database. Automation risk is limited to adding a doctor state migration entry. Provider-data signals come from copying existing embedding cache columns, not changing provider authentication, routing, or request signing.
- **Why acceptable:** The import only runs through the explicit doctor migration path, validates legacy sidecar schema shape before copying rows, restores matching legacy vector rows into the canonical sqlite-vec table before archive, skips overwrite when canonical index rows already exist, and archives handled sidecar files to prevent stale startup reads.
- **Patch quality notes:** The broad catches are bounded to migration discovery/per-source reporting so one unreadable legacy source does not abort unrelated doctor migrations; the schema import catch rolls back a savepoint and rethrows. The `return null` path is the existing doctor contract for no detected legacy state. The larger diff is mostly SQLite fixture and regression coverage needed to prove the persisted-state migration boundary.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 88/100; the remaining evidence boundary is that this was verified locally with real SQLite state files rather than a packaged release-beta upgrade install, external provider run, or reporter-state proof.
